movies.title: Inception
movies.review: A mind-bending thriller that challenges the boundaries of reality and dreams. Christopher Nolan at his finest.

movies.title: The Dark Knight
movies.review: A masterpiece of superhero cinema. Heath Ledger's Joker is iconic and the story is deeply compelling.

movies.title: The Shining
movies.review: Kubrick's chilling adaptation of Stephen King's novel remains one of the greatest horror films ever made.

movies.title: The Grand Budapest Hotel
movies.review: Wes Anderson's whimsical masterpiece bursting with color, wit, and an unforgettable ensemble cast.

movies.title: Interstellar
movies.review: An emotionally devastating and visually stunning space epic that explores love transcending dimensions.

movies.title: Parasite
movies.review: Bong Joon-ho's genre-defying masterwork on class inequality that utterly subverts expectations.

movies.title: Mad Max: Fury Road
movies.review: A relentless, visually spectacular post-apocalyptic chase film that redefined action cinema.

movies.title: A Quiet Place
movies.review: A brilliantly tense survival horror that uses silence as its greatest weapon. Deeply emotional.

movies.title: The Wolf of Wall Street
movies.review: DiCaprio is electric in this wild, excess-fueled ride through Wall Street corruption. Scorsese at his most energetic.

movies.title: Raiders of the Lost Ark
movies.review: The quintessential adventure film that launched one of cinema's greatest franchises. Pure cinematic joy.

movies.title: Get Out
movies.review: Jordan Peele's razor-sharp social commentary wrapped in a terrifying psychological horror package.

movies.title: Avengers: Endgame
movies.review: An unprecedented culmination of a decade of Marvel storytelling. Emotionally resonant and spectacularly entertaining.

movies.title: The Martian
movies.review: Matt Damon is perfectly cast in this optimistic, witty survival story set on Mars. Science has never been this fun.

movies.title: Knives Out
movies.review: A deliciously clever whodunit with a stellar ensemble cast and genuine subversive surprises.

movies.title: Superbad
movies.review: The ultimate coming-of-age comedy. Hilariously raunchy yet surprisingly heartfelt about the end of an era.
Sam Mendes crafts an astonishing single-take illusion of a WWI mission. Heart-stopping from start to finish.

movies.title: The Jungle Book
movies.review: A stunning visual achievement that breathes new life into the classic tale with remarkable CGI and voice work.

movies.title: Prisoners
movies.review: A devastating, morally complex thriller driven by Hugh Jackman and Jake Gyllenhaal's powerhouse performances.

movies.title: The Truman Show
movies.review: Jim Carrey's dramatic turn is brilliant in this prescient, thoughtful exploration of reality and surveillance.

movies.title: Everything Everywhere All at Once
movies.review: A maximalist multiverse odyssey that somehow turns absurdist chaos into the most moving film about family in years.

movies.title: John Wick
movies.review: Keanu Reeves is unstoppable in this slick, stylishly choreographed revenge thriller that spawned a franchise.

movies.title: Midsommar
movies.review: Ari Aster's folk horror masterpiece that unsettles in broad daylight. Visually unforgettable and deeply disturbing.
